`write_font(None, ...)` short-circuit (line 207). **`pypdfbox/pdmodel/fixup/processor/acro_form_orphan_widgets_processor.py` 96.4% ‚Üí 100%**: `handle_annotations` `ImportError` swallow (lines 103-104) and `resolve_non_root_field` `ImportError` swallow (lines 174-175) ‚Äî both reached via a monkey-patched `builtins.__import__` that raises on the lazy `PDAnnotationWidget` / `PDFieldFactory` imports. **`pypdfbox/pdmodel/graphics/shading/radial_shading_context.py` 97.0% ‚Üí 98%** (98% reported ‚Äî see latent bug below): high-input no-extend / no-bg `continue` (line 165) via `extend=(True, False)` so the elif chain selects `r0 > 1` then the high-side block falls through `elif bg is None: continue`; low-input extend[0]-with-`r0==0` `use_background = True` (line 174) and its mirror high-input extend[1]-with-`r1==0` (line 174) ‚Äî both with `coords[2]=0` or `coords[5]=0` forcing the first clause to fail and bg present. **`pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/annotation/handlers/pd_line_appearance_handler.py` 97.6% ‚Üí 100%**: `paths_array is None` early-return (line 49) by removing `/L` from a freshly-constructed `PDAnnotationLine`; caption-emit `(AttributeError, ValueError)` swallow (lines 182-184) by monkey-patching `PDPageContentStream.begin_text` to raise `AttributeError` so the catch fires without leaving the content stream in BT-mode; `_interior_components` `size()` branch (line 269) via a lazy `__getattr__` stand-in that hides `to_float_array` from the outer `hasattr` probe yet resolves it inside the elif arm. **`pypdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/annotation/handlers/pd_polyline_appearance_handler.py` 96.7% ‚Üí 100%**: `cs.set_dash_pattern` emission (line 80) via a `PDBorderStyleDictionary` with `STYLE_DASHED` + non-zero `/D` dash array ‚Äî verified through the `d` operator in the resulting appearance-stream bytes; `_interior_components` `size()` branch (lines 170-172) via the same lazy-`__getattr__` pattern as the line handler. **Latent bugs flagged:** (1) `RadialShadingContext.get_raster` clamps `input_value` to literal `0` / `1` on the extend branches (lines 163, 170) rather than `self._domain[0]` / `self._domain[1]` as the sibling `AxialShadingContext` does ‚Äî this leaves the post-key clamps at lines 180 / 182 (`if key < 0: key = 0` / `elif key > factor: key = factor`) structurally dead. Upstream Java RadialShadingContext likely uses domain values; needs an upstream cross-check before fixing. (2) `PDLineAppearanceHandler.generate_normal_appearance` catches `(AttributeError, ValueError)` around the caption emission block (lines 173-184) but does *not* call `cs.end_text()` in the except clause; if `cs.show_text` raises mid-BT, the subsequent `cs.restore_graphics_state()` at line 201 then raises `RuntimeError: restore_graphics_state is not allowed within a text block`. The test deliberately patches `begin_text` (pre-BT) to avoid this latent crash. (20 tests) |

time = 0` arm that mirrors upstream's `createUID(URL u)` overload (1 test). **Latent bug flagged:** `tests/examples/interactive/form/test_print_fields.py::test_process_field_handles_value_exception` (wave pre-1351) silently fails to exercise the exception branch it claims to cover ‚Äî `field.get_value_as_string = _raise` monkey-patches the instance, but `PDAcroForm.get_fields()` constructs fresh wrappers from the COS tree on every call, so the patched method is discarded before `print_fields` dispatches into `process_field`. The new wave-1351 test bypasses `print_fields` and calls `process_field` directly with a `Mock` field so the exception path is actually executed. The original test still asserts on output strings and passes coincidentally because the bare `PDTextField` returns an empty value via the happy path. |

Pinned the backdrop to ``"RGB"`` and re-attach the original alpha after subtraction. The fix would be to gate the flip on a successful close (e.g. `else:` or a success flag). Any caller running `PDType1FontEmbedder.__init__` in a fresh process before some other test has triggered the defensive registration would hit `AttributeError: type object 'COSName' has no attribute 'FONT_DESC'`. The fix is a three-line registration in `cos_name.py` at module bottom (next to the existing `COSName.WIDTHS = _static_name("Widths")` block); kept out of this wave because it touches production source. No `# pragma: no cover` markers added. |

`filter/jbig2_filter.py` lines 112-113 (defensive `FilterFactory.register` try/except guard); `filter/jpx_filter.py` lines 82-83 (same defensive registration guard); `filter/jpx_decode.py` line 214 (`pixels == 0 or bytes_per_sample == 0` ‚Äî guarded above). **One latent observation**: the `# type: ignore[unreachable]` on `non_seekable_random_access_read_input_stream.py` line 125 was already present and is correct under static type-checking; the runtime guard is dead but mirrors upstream's Java `Objects.requireNonNull` defensiveness. No latent source bugs flagged. |
| `tests/debugger/test_pd_debugger_print_wave1359.py` | 3.0.x | hand-written behaviour-feature tests for the Debugger Print menu (wave 1359 agent C). 10 tests covering `_print_menu_item_action_performed` (no-document silent return, empty-document info dialog, populated-document spooler dispatch, helper-raises error dialog, `get_number_of_pages` failure surfaced via showerror) and the new `_send_document_to_printer` helper across its three OS-dispatch arms (POSIX `lp` writes a valid 3-page temp PDF; Windows `os.startfile(path, 'print')`; `open` / `xdg-open` fallback when no spooler; `Popen('lp', ...)` OSError falls through to opener; final no-spooler-no-opener info dialog points the user at the rasterised PDF). The Print menu previously surfaced a 'not implemented' messagebox; this wave wires it to the host OS print pipeline by rasterising every page via `PDFRenderer.render_image_with_dpi(dpi=150)` and bundling the images into a temp multi-page PDF via Pillow's `Image.save(save_all=True, append_images=...)`. No new dependencies. |

| `tests/pdmodel/interactive/pagenavigation/upstream/test_pd_transition.py` | 3.0.x | `pdfbox/src/test/java/org/apache/pdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/pagenavigation/PDTransitionTest.java` (wave 1363 agent D; 10 tests ‚Äî default `/Type /Trans` stamping, default `/S /R`, default `/Di` zero, set/get for dimension/motion/duration/fly-scale, `/Di` `/None` name vs `/Di 0` integer dispatch via `set_direction`, fly-area-opaque toggle. | `tests/pdmodel/interactive/form/upstream/test_pd_acro_form_flatten.py` | 3.0.x | `pdfbox/src/test/java/org/apache/pdfbox/pdmodel/interactive/form/PDAcroFormFlattenTest.java` (wave 1363 agent D; 1 active test + 12 skip-placeholders. The active path is `flattenSingleField` against the bundled `MultilineFields.pdf` fixture ‚Äî flattens one named text field via `acro_form.flatten([field], False)` and asserts the `/Fields` count drops by one and the named lookup returns `None`. The 10 parametrised render-compare matrix entries, `flattenTestPDFBOX5254`, and `flattenTestPDFBOX5225` are skipped because each fetches a sample PDF from Apache JIRA over HTTPS and compares to a renderer-generated PNG reference ‚Äî both network access and pixel-perfect renderer parity are outside the AcroForm flatten harness's responsibility) |

The Java-only `System.out.println(dash)` line at the end of the upstream test is replaced with a direct `str(dash)` assertion so the diagnostic surface is pinned rather than silently dropped) |
| `tests/pdmodel/graphics/upstream/test_blend_mode_channels.py` | 3.0.x | `pdfbox/src/test/java/org/apache/pdfbox/pdmodel/graphics/blend/BlendModeTest.java` (wave 1363 agent C; 17 tests ‚Äî completes the upstream test class by porting the per-mode channel assertions (`get_blend_function` / `get_blend_channel_function` / `get_cos_name`) plus `testInstances`. The companion file `test_blend_mode.py` already covered `get_instance` dispatch + HSL helpers. Adds parity checks for the COLOR_DODGE/COLOR_BURN PDF 2.0 algorithm against `(s=1,b=0)` and `(s=0,b=1)` corner cases that revealed a latent bug in the previous PDF-1.7-only implementation) |
| `tests/pdmodel/graphics/image/upstream/test_png_converter.py` | 3.0.x | `pdfbox/src/test/java/org/apache/pdfbox/pdmodel/graphics/image/PNGConverterTest.java` (wave 1363 agent C; 4 active + 1 skip ‚Äî ports `testChunkSane`, `testCheckConverterState`, `testCRCImpl`, `testMapPNGRenderIntent`. The image-conversion tests (`testImageConversionRGB`, `...RGBGamma`, `...RGB16BitICC`, `...RGBIndexed`, the 4-variant `...RGBIndexedAlpha[1248]Bit`, `testImageConversionIntentIndexed` and the failure-path counterparts `testImageConversionRGBAlpha` / `...GrayAlpha` / `...Gray` / `...GrayGamma`) need binary PNG fixtures that pypdfbox does not bundle ‚Äî equivalent end-to-end coverage already lives in `tests/pdmodel/graphics/image/test_png_converter_coverage.py` against Pillow-generated PNGs, so we keep a single documented skip rather than re-pinning the same coverage with raw upstream bytes. Adds parity checks that revealed and resolved two latent bugs in `PNGConverter.check_chunk_sane` (rebuilt the 4-byte type prefix from `chunk_type` instead of slicing it from `bytes_[start-4:start]`) and `PNGConverter.check_converter_state` (only checked existence of IHDR/IDATs and width/height>0, never validated optional chunks or per-IDAT CRC)) |
| `tests/fontbox/cff/upstream/test_cff_charset.py` | 3.0.x | `fontbox/src/test/java/org/apache/fontbox/cff/CFFCharsetTest.java` (wave 1363 agent E; 6 tests ‚Äî full port covering the embedded CID/Type1 dispatcher (`EmbeddedCharset`), the concrete `CFFCharsetCID` / `CFFCharsetType1` flavours, and the three singleton built-in charsets (`CFFExpertCharset`, `CFFExpertSubsetCharset`, `CFFISOAdobeCharset`) round-trips. Java's `IllegalStateException` on type-mismatched operations maps to Python `RuntimeError` per the existing pypdfbox.fontbox.cff convention) |

| `tests/fontbox/ttf/gsub/upstream/test_gsub_worker_for_latin.py` | 3.0.x | `fontbox/src/test/java/org/apache/fontbox/ttf/gsub/GsubWorkerForLatinTest.java` (wave 1363 agent E; 2 skip-placeholders ‚Äî Calibri is Windows-proprietary (upstream itself only runs that case via `Assumptions.assumeTrue` on Windows dev machines) and FoglihtenNo07.otf is not bundled (custom non-Apache license per the wave 1360 agent C policy note). `GsubWorkerForLatin` is covered by `tests/fontbox/ttf/gsub/test_gsub_worker_for_latin.py` and `tests/fontbox/ttf/upstream/test_glyph_substitution_table_liberation_font.py`) |
| `tests/tools/upstream/test_pdf_text2_html.py` | 3.0.x | `tools/src/test/java/org/apache/pdfbox/tools/TestPDFText2HTML.java` (wave 1363 agent E; 2 tests ‚Äî `escape_title` and `style`. Drives a real `PDDocument` + `PDPageContentStream` + Helvetica/Helvetica-Bold render through `PDFText2HTML.get_text` (no monkeypatch) so the end-to-end HTML wrapping path is exercised. Surfaced and fixed three latent source bugs: (a) `PDFText2HTML.start_document` / `end_document` / `start_article` / `end_article` / `write_string` / `write_paragraph_end` called `super().write_string(...)` with a 1-arg signature that no longer existed on the parent (3-arg `(text, text_positions, sink)`); (b) `PDFText2HTML.get_title` read `self.document` which pypdfbox's parent does not expose; (c) `PDFont.load_font_descriptor` returned `None` for Standard 14 fonts even though upstream synthesizes a descriptor from the AFM in that case, breaking bold/italic detection. See CHANGES.md wave 1363 agent E for the full repair) |
| `tests/fontbox/cmap/upstream/test_codespace_range.py` | 3.0.x | `fontbox/src/test/java/org/apache/fontbox/cmap/TestCodespaceRange.java` (wave 1364 agent E; 3 tests ‚Äî code-length calculation, the PDFBOX-4923 mixed-length-constructor relaxation that accepts `<00> <ffff>`, and rectangular `matches` checks for single- and two-byte ranges. `IllegalArgumentException` ‚Üí `ValueError`) |
| `tests/util/upstream/test_matrix.py` | 3.0.x | `pdfbox/src/test/java/org/apache/pdfbox/util/MatrixTest.java` (wave 1364 agent D; 13 tests ‚Äî full 1:1 port of `MatrixTest`. Covers identity / clone, scaling-factor, `create_matrix` rejection of non-`COSArray` / short / non-numeric inputs, the three-way multiplication contract (`multiply` returns new, `concatenate` in-place, static `concatenate_matrices`), the old-multiplication regression, the four illegal-value paths (`Float.MAX_VALUE` analogue, `NaN`, `+/-Infinity`) which check that `Matrix.multiply` rejects non-finite intermediates via `ValueError`, the PDFBOX-2872 `to_cos_array` six-float regression, `get_values`, `scale`, and `translate`. The performance benchmark `testMultiplicationPerformance` is omitted (commented-out `@Test` upstream). `IllegalArgumentException` ‚Üí `ValueError` per pypdfbox-wide convention) |

Authored against a live PDFBox 3.0.7 differential run (`oracle/probes/PredictorDecodeProbe.java` + `PredictorProbe.java`, 1150+ random geometries) which found ZERO divergence ‚Äî no production bug; the tests freeze that parity |

### Wave 1454 additions ‚Äî live-oracle differential probes + tests

| File | Upstream version | Upstream Java path / note |
|---|---|---|
| `oracle/probes/PageInheritanceProbe.java` | 3.0.x | hand-written live-oracle probe (wave 1454); emits resolved `/MediaBox`, `/CropBox`, `/Rotate`, and `/Resources` `/Font` + `/XObject` sub-counts per page to differentially pin pypdfbox's page-tree attribute inheritance (PDF ¬ß7.7.3.4) against PDFBox 3.0.7's `PDPage.getMediaBox/getCropBox/getRotation/getResources`; no single upstream JUnit source |
| `tests/pdmodel/oracle/test_page_inheritance_oracle.py` | 3.0.x | hand-written live-oracle differential (probe `oracle/probes/PageInheritanceProbe.java`, wave 1454); seven focused cases for `/MediaBox` root-inheritance, intermediate-override, default-letter-fallback, `/CropBox` defaults to resolved `/MediaBox`, `/Rotate` multiples-of-90 sanitisation, `/Resources` inheritance, and the negative sibling-isolation case. Surface confirmed at parity; surfaces a deferred high-blast-radius `PDPage.get_resources` null-vs-empty divergence tracked in `DEFERRED.md`. No single upstream JUnit source |
| `oracle/probes/DestinationTypesProbe.java` | 3.0.x | hand-written live-oracle probe (wave 1454); for each `/Outlines` entry emits `dest_type` class-name, `page_index`, and the `left/top/right/bottom/zoom` accessor tuple to differentially pin PDFBox 3.0.7's eight `PDPageDestination` subclass dispatch + the two name-tree resolution surfaces (`/Names /Dests`, legacy `/Dests`); no single upstream JUnit source |
| `tests/pdmodel/interactive/oracle/test_destination_types_oracle.py` | 3.0.x | hand-written live-oracle differential (probe `oracle/probes/DestinationTypesProbe.java`, wave 1454); 9 cases covering all 8 explicit destination array syntaxes (`/XYZ`, `/Fit`, `/FitB`, `/FitH`, `/FitBH`, `/FitV`, `/FitBV`, `/FitR`) and both name-resolution surfaces. Surface confirmed at parity. No single upstream JUnit source |
| `oracle/probes/OutlineTreeProbe.java` | 3.0.x | hand-written live-oracle probe (wave 1454); emits nested `title` (PDFDocEncoding/UTF-16BE-decoded), signed `count`, resolved `dest` (page index), and `child_count` for the outline tree to differentially pin PDFBox 3.0.7's `PDOutlineNode.children()` + `getOpenCount()` + `getTitle()` + destination resolution (`/Dest` explicit/named + `/A GoTo` explicit/named); no single upstream JUnit source |
| `tests/pdmodel/interactive/oracle/test_outline_tree_oracle.py` | 3.0.x | hand-written live-oracle differential (probe `oracle/probes/OutlineTreeProbe.java`, wave 1454); 5 cases covering `add_last`+`open_node` `/Count` propagation, full destination-resolution matrix, unicode title decoding, cyclic `/Next` safety, and missing-outline null. Surface confirmed at parity. No single upstream JUnit source |
| `oracle/probes/EmbeddedFilesMultiProbe.java` | 3.0.x | hand-written live-oracle probe (wave 1454); emits per-attachment `name`, `f_filename`, `uf_filename`, `desc`, `af_relationship`, byte-length, MD5 (when `/Params /CheckSum` present) plus the kid-tree structural shape (leaf/kid/total counts) for the catalog `/Names /EmbeddedFiles` name tree; no single upstream JUnit source |

Surface confirmed at parity. No single upstream JUnit source |
| `oracle/probes/ViewerPrefsAndOpenActionProbe.java` | 3.0.x | hand-written live-oracle probe (wave 1454); orthogonal extension to the existing `ViewerPrefsProbe` ‚Äî emits the four boundary getters (`/ViewArea`/`/ViewClip`/`/PrintArea`/`/PrintClip` ‚Äî default `CropBox` baked into PDFBox's getter), the PDF 32000-2 enrichment entries (`/PickTrayByPDFSize`/`/NumCopies`/`/PrintPageRange`/`/Enforce`), and the catalog `/OpenAction` kind+subtype dispatch (destination XYZ/Fit/etc. vs action GoTo/URI/etc., dict-without-`/S`, COSName/COSString shorthand); no single upstream JUnit source |

BUG FIXED in this wave ‚Äî `PDDocumentCatalog.get_open_action` was delegating to the looser `PDDestinationOrAction.create` factory, which (a) accepted `COSName`/`COSString` `/OpenAction` as a `PDNamedDestination` shorthand and (b) treated a dict that lacked `/S` but carried `/D` as an implicit `PDActionGoTo`. Upstream `PDDocumentCatalog.getOpenAction()` switches on `COSDictionary`/`COSArray` only and uses `PDActionFactory.createAction` which returns `null` for missing `/S` ‚Äî both arms now resolve to `None`. No single upstream JUnit source |

BUG FIXED ‚Äî `get_widths()` SKIPPED non-numeric `/Widths` entries, shifting every later index; upstream `COSArray.toCOSNumberFloatList` keeps a `None` slot in place (read back as 0.0). `PDFont`/`PDSimpleFont.get_widths` now return `list[float | None]` with `None` for non-numeric slots; all array consumers (Type1/Type1C/TrueType/Type3 widths + averages + Type3 renderer advance) null-guarded; no single upstream JUnit source |

BUG FIXED ‚Äî `PDType0Font.code_to_gid` assumed `GID == CID` for a descendant lacking `/CIDToGIDMap`; for a CIDFontType0 (CID-keyed CFF) the GID is the charset index (CID 40‚ÜíGID 10), so it now delegates to the descendant's charset-based `code_to_gid` (matching upstream `descendantFont.codeToGID`); no single upstream JUnit source |

| `oracle/probes/PredefCMapInfoProbe.java` | 3.0.7 | hand-written live-oracle probe (wave 1470 agent H); `org/apache/fontbox/cmap/CMap.java` metadata getters; no single upstream JUnit source |

| `oracle/probes/ColorKeyMaskProbe.java` | 3.0.7 | hand-written live-oracle probe (wave 1470 agent I); `org/apache/pdfbox/pdmodel/graphics/image/SampledImageReader.java` color-key `/Mask` on non-RGB images; no single upstream JUnit source |
| `tests/pdmodel/graphics/image/oracle/test_color_key_mask_nonrgb_oracle.py` | 3.0.7 | hand-written live-oracle differential (probe `oracle/probes/ColorKeyMaskProbe.java`, wave 1470 agent I); color-key `/Mask` (¬ß8.9.6.4) on DeviceGray/DeviceCMYK/Indexed. BUG FIXED ‚Äî `_apply_color_key_mask` compared the converted sRGB pixels against the range pairs and required ‚â•6 entries, so 1-pair (Gray/Indexed) keys were silently ignored and CMYK keyed the wrong components; now keys against the raw native samples per component (Indexed honours its index `/Decode`); RGB path unchanged; no single upstream JUnit source |
| `oracle/probes/PdfToImageProbe.java` | 3.0.7 | hand-written live-oracle probe (wave 1470 agent J); `pdfbox/tools/.../PDFToImage.java` render loop; no single upstream JUnit source |

BUG FIXED ‚Äî `render_image_with_dpi` computed raster pixel dims in Python double (`int(width_pt * dpi/72.0)`); upstream uses Java float (`Math.floor(widthPt * (dpi/72f))`), so 792pt@150dpi ‚Üí 1649 not 1650; now computes dims in `numpy.float32` (device CTM geometry kept double-precision); no single upstream JUnit source |

## External font assets (runtime-fetched, never bundled)

These are **not** Apache PDFBox ports ‚Äî they are upstream font binaries
that the opt-in :mod:`pypdfbox.fontbox.cjk_loader` may fetch on the
user's behalf when both `pip install pypdfbox[cjk]` and
`PYPDFBOX_CJK_AUTODOWNLOAD=1` are set. The loader verifies the SHA-256
below before extraction and never redistributes the binaries through
pypdfbox itself.

Upstream project: <https://github.com/notofonts/noto-cjk>
Upstream release tag: `Sans2.004` (2022-01-27)
License: SIL Open Font License 1.1 (permissive; on the project allow-list)

| asset (release) | SHA-256 | extracted font (Regular weight only) |
|---|---|---|
| `16_NotoSansJP.zip` | `2bbdd2c20f30670b39ca735c96d75f1fdabdb348103e43b820cf17701fd22b18` | `NotoSansJP-Regular.otf` |
| `17_NotoSansKR.zip` | `ac7eeb4e2b0d41de8ff31b2d6e1e2a41caf253fd5cefb380bfa1f40f1747b612` | `NotoSansKR-Regular.otf` |
| `18_NotoSansSC.zip` | `4d107c09ada479d3e48b6e78c83835773cbd9214bf6e12cdb7b60f8e068292ec` | `NotoSansSC-Regular.otf` |
| `19_NotoSansTC.zip` | `fbbcb216be8056a436c7ec142847f302bb1932d07bdad8b322f4953a389d7cbc` | `NotoSansTC-Regular.otf` |
| `20_NotoSansHK.zip` | `c2d8a17fc668116920b75a3bc6f63606faefe43722efec7bb03c66164b3ec52f` | `NotoSansHK-Regular.otf` |

| `oracle/probes/FunctionType3FuzzProbe.java` | 3.0.7 | hand-written live-oracle probe (wave 1523 agent A); malformed Type 3 (stitching) function dictionaries over `org/apache/pdfbox/pdmodel/common/function/PDFunctionType3.java` (`eval`) ‚Äî missing/empty/non-array `/Functions`, single vs multi dispatch, `/Bounds` length/order/out-of-Domain, `/Encode` length/non-numeric, input clamping + boundary interval selection, zero-width subdomain, malformed sub-function, `/Domain` corners; no single upstream JUnit source |

| `oracle/probes/FunctionType2FuzzProbe.java` | 3.0.7 | hand-written live-oracle probe (wave 1536 agent D); malformed Type 2 (exponential) function dictionaries over `org/apache/pdfbox/pdmodel/common/function/PDFunctionType2.java` (`eval`, constructor C0/C1 defaulting) ‚Äî `/N` = 1/huge/fractional/negative-even, `x=0` with negative `/N` (`Math.pow(0,neg)`=+Infinity), present-but-empty `/C0`//`/C1` (constructor materialises `[0]`/`[1]`), C0/C1 length mismatch (min sizing), multi-component interpolation, missing/reversed `/Domain`, input outside `/Domain` (no input clip), `/Range` per-component clipping, negative base with fractional `/N`; no single upstream JUnit source |

| `oracle/probes/CosNameHexEscapeFuzzProbe.java` | 3.0.7 | hand-written live-oracle probe (wave 1523 agent C); COSName `#XX` hex-escape decode + byte‚ÜíString round-trip over `org.apache.pdfbox.cos.COSName#getName` via `org.apache.pdfbox.pdfparser.BaseParser#parseCOSName` (the `ALTERNATIVE_CHARSET` = Windows-1252 fallback); no single upstream JUnit source |

| `oracle/probes/RectangleFuzzProbe.java` | 3.0.7 | hand-written live-oracle probe (wave 1524 agent D); malformed `PDRectangle(COSArray)` construction over `org/apache/pdfbox/pdmodel/common/PDRectangle.java` ‚Äî wrong-length arrays (0/1/2/3/5/6 entries), non-numeric slots (COSName/COSString/COSNull), reversed/negative corners, COSFloat/COSInteger mix, overflow magnitudes (clamp), indirect-reference slots, plus the `getLowerLeftX/Y`/`getUpperRightX/Y`/`getWidth`/`getHeight`/`contains`/`createRetranslatedRectangle`/`getCOSArray` accessors; no single upstream JUnit source |
| `tests/pdmodel/oracle/test_rectangle_fuzz_wave1524.py` | 3.0.7 | hand-written live-oracle differential (probe `oracle/probes/RectangleFuzzProbe.java`, wave 1524 agent D); 29 cases; fixed `from_cos_array` to zero-pad/truncate to 4 entries, treat non-numeric/null slots (and indirect refs resolving to non-numbers) as 0.0 instead of raising, resolve indirect numerics, and clamp huge magnitudes at `(float) Integer.MAX_VALUE` = 2147483648.0; pins the float32-vs-float64 width/height cliff at the overflow boundary; no single upstream JUnit source |
| `oracle/probes/StandardSecurityHandlerFuzzProbe.java` | 3.0.7 | hand-written live-oracle probe (wave 1524 agent E); in-process differential fuzz of `StandardSecurityHandler` key derivation + password auth (`computeEncryptedKey`, `isUserPassword`, `isOwnerPassword`, `getUserPassword`) over `org/apache/pdfbox/pdmodel/encryption/StandardSecurityHandler.java` ‚Äî deterministic byte inputs with malformed `/O` `/U` `/OE` `/UE`, out-of-range `/R` (0/7/99), odd `/Length` (0/5/16), short/empty/over-long entries, >32-byte passwords; emits `CASE <name> <key=hex \| user=0\|1 \| owner=0\|1 \| getuser=hex \| ERR:<Exc>>`; no single upstream JUnit source |
| `tests/pdmodel/encryption/oracle/test_standard_security_handler_fuzz_wave1524.py` | 3.0.7 | hand-written live-oracle differential (probe `oracle/probes/StandardSecurityHandlerFuzzProbe.java`, wave 1524 agent E); 55 cases (43 byte-exact parity + 12 pinned); fixed three `>=`-vs-exact-revision divergences in `standard_security_handler.py` (`compute_encrypted_key` Rev56 dispatch ‚Üí `revision in (5,6)`; Algorithm 2 50-round loop ‚Üí `revision in (3,4)` and `0xFFFFFFFF` metadata mix ‚Üí `revision == 4`; `get_user_password` r3/r4 unwind guarded on `{2,3,4}`); pins `IOException`‚Üî`OSError`, `IllegalArgumentException`‚Üî`ValueError`, and Java `ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException`‚Üîpypdfbox lenient-slice divergences; no single upstream JUnit source |
| `oracle/probes/DecryptDataFuzzProbe.java` | 3.0.7 | hand-written live-oracle probe (wave 1532 agent E); reflection-driven differential fuzz of the private `SecurityHandler.encryptData(objNum, genNum, InputStream, OutputStream, decrypt)` cipher DISPATCH over `org/apache/pdfbox/pdmodel/encryption/SecurityHandler.java` ‚Äî the single funnel routing `useAES && key.length==32` ‚Üí AES-256 (file key, 16-byte IV), `useAES` ‚Üí AES-other (per-object key), else RC4; deterministic fixed keys + malformed ciphertext (partial IV, IV-only, non-block-multiple, empty, bad padding, AES-256 vs AES-128 routing, RC4 empty/short, zero-length key); emits `CASE <name> <out=hex \| ERR:<Exc>>`; no single upstream JUnit source |
| `tests/pdmodel/encryption/oracle/test_decrypt_data_fuzz_wave1532.py` | 3.0.7 | hand-written live-oracle differential (probe `oracle/probes/DecryptDataFuzzProbe.java`, wave 1532 agent E); 20 cases (15 byte-exact parity + 5 pinned exception-class divergences); fixed three real divergences in `security_handler.py` (`_decrypt`/`_encrypt` route AES-256 by `_aes and len(key)==32` not `revision>=5`; `_aes_cbc_decrypt` raises on a partial IV `0<n<16` instead of returning empty; bad PKCS#7 padding raises in the AES-128 per-object path but is silently dropped block-wise in the AES-256 `CipherInputStream` path); pins Java `IOException`‚Üî`OSError` and `InvalidKeyException`‚Üî`ValueError`; no single upstream JUnit source |
